assertions.expectation: Explains that multi-host TPU slices are recreated atomically in a single step — not a rolling upgrade
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'All slices restart simultaneously' and 'No rolling updates - Unlike standard node pools, there's no gradual node-by-node replacement' and 'atomic recreation behavior'

assertions.expectation: Warns that a maintenance event on ANY single slice triggers restart of ALL slices in the multislice environment
assertions.reason: Response clearly states 'Cascading restart trigger - A maintenance event on ANY slice triggers restart of ALL slices in the multislice environment'

assertions.expectation: Notes that all pods on all affected slices will restart simultaneously — the training job will be interrupted
assertions.reason: Response states 'Complete job termination - Your LLM training job will be forcibly terminated when the slices are recreated' and 'All training pods will be terminated immediately'

assertions.expectation: Explains that if insufficient TPU resources are available during recreation, the slice blocks until hardware is freed — potentially causing extended downtime
assertions.reason: Response mentions downtime expectations (15-35 minutes) but doesn't specifically address the scenario of insufficient TPU resources causing extended blocking during recreation

assertions.expectation: Recommends saving a training checkpoint immediately before the maintenance window
assertions.reason: Response includes 'Checkpoint save scheduled 30 minutes before maintenance window' in checklist and provides specific pre-maintenance checkpoint commands

assertions.expectation: Recommends configuring the training framework for automatic checkpoint resume so it can restart after the maintenance event
assertions.reason: Response includes 'Review training script for graceful restart behavior' in checklist and provides detailed checkpoint restore code examples with 'initialize_or_restore()' function

assertions.expectation: Suggests using maintenance exclusions to defer the event if the training run is close to completion
assertions.reason: Response includes a section on 'Maintenance Exclusion Strategy' with specific gcloud commands, though it notes limited effectiveness for TPU infrastructure maintenance

assertions.expectation: Mentions maintenance windows to control when the event occurs — schedule it during a planned break in training
assertions.reason: Response mentions maintenance windows in the checklist ('Maintenance notification received with specific timing') but doesn't explain how to configure or use maintenance windows to schedule the event during planned breaks
